The type field categorizes the log event, enabling event type filtering and grouping.
The process ID identifies the process generating the log, enabling process lineage and session correlation.
The user ID identifies the user account under which the process ran, aiding in user-centric session grouping.
The audit user ID represents the original logged-in user, supporting audit trail and user impersonation detection.
The session ID groups related log events into a single user session for session-level correlation.
The message field contains a nested payload with operation-specific details for deep event analysis.
op=PAM:session_open acct="root" exe="/usr/sbin/cron" hostname=? addr=? terminal=cron res=success
The operation field describes the type of PAM operation performed, enabling event action classification.
The account field specifies the user account name involved in the operation, aiding in user activity tracking.
The executable field indicates the program path that initiated the operation, enabling process-to-entity linkage.
The terminal field identifies the terminal or service associated with the session, supporting origin-based grouping.
The result field indicates the outcome of the operation (e.g., success or failure), aiding in event status analysis.
